name: All You Zombies Robert Heinlein
desc: It's a book about zombies. Scary, haunted eyes. Maybe. It's got almost all of it scribbled on with black marker.

name: Insectopedia Hugh Raffles
desc: <style=prompt>"Perhaps the most dramatic example of parasitic brain control is the fungus Ophiocordyceps, which turns ants into "zombies" to ensure its own survival. A fungal spore lands on an ant, germinating and growing throughout its body, using the ant's own muscles to turn it into a puppet. As the infection reaches the brain, the fungus causes the ant to abandon its colony, climb to a specific height on a plant, and bite down on a leaf with a "fatal grip". Once in this optimal, humid position for fungal growth, the parasite kills the host and grows a spore-releasing stalk out of the ant's head to infect others."</style>

name: Stocks Stiff Peaks
desc: It's boring. It's one of Mom's books - it looks like it's the same color and has the same art on the cover.

name: The Blind Owl Sadegh Hedayat
desc: The faces on the cover are scribbled out, even the owl. It's also mostly blacked out with marker. The marker looks purple in the lamp light.

name: <style=prompt><align=center>Chess Board</align></style>
desc: It's a chess board, or a checkers board. There are no pieces.

name: <style=prompt><align=center>Pen Case</align></style>
desc: It had a pen once. Dad broke it against the wall a a long, long time ago.

name: <style=prompt><align=center>Photo Frame</align></style>
desc: It's an empty photo frame. It wasn't empty before. Why didn't they just scribble over the faces like in the other books?
